02 March 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Transcript: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine Hold a Press Briefing DoD 02 Feb 2026
  • Hegseth Says 'Epic Fury' Goals in Iran Are 'Laser-Focused' DoD News 02 Mar 2026 -- Since early morning Feb. 28, American forces in the U.S. Central Command area of operations have been involved in an intense bombing campaign against Iran that has already resulted in the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
  • US Will 'Go As Far As We Need To Go' In Iran Conflict, Pentagon Chief Vows RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- The United States signaled it would press ahead with its aerial assault on Iran, as Tehran continued to volley missiles and drones at targets across the Gulf and Middle East amid fears the conflict could widen beyond the region.
  • 3 US Fighter Jets Downed By Friendly Fire Over Kuwait, Officials Say RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- US military officials said three fighter jets crashed in Kuwait after being hit by friendly fire from Kuwaiti air defenses, the first downing of US aircraft since the US-Israeli war against Iran began over the weekend.
  • Three U.S. F-15s Involved in Friendly Fire Incident in Kuwait; Pilots Safe CENTCOM 02 Mar 2026 -- At 11:03 p.m. ET, March 1, three U.S. F-15E Strike Eagles flying in support of Operation Epic Fury went down over Kuwait due to an apparent friendly fire incident.
  • Operational concerns delayed US-Israeli aggression against Iran for a week: Report Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- The United States and Israel had originally planned to launch their military strike on Iran about a week earlier than it eventually took place, but the aggression was delayed due to a mix of operational concerns, intelligence assessments and diplomatic developments, according to a report.

  • Operation Roaring Lion - Israel MOD Launched an Aerial and Maritime Airlift to Deliver Military Equipment and Munitions to the IDF Israel MOD 02 Mar 2026
  • Statement by PM Netanyahu at the Site of the Missile Strike in Beit Shemesh Israel - Prime Minister 02 Mar 2026

  • IRGC targets US intelligence centers, military depots in 11th wave of attacks Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian armed forces have started a new wave of extensive drone and missile attacks on targets in the Israeli-occupied territories and US assets in regional countries.
  • First US fighter downed in past 27 years: Iran Armed Forces hit F-15 near Kuwait border Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Armed Forces have shot down an advanced US F-15 fighter jet near the border with Kuwait - the first downing of an American fighter jet in the past 27 years.
  • IRGC, Army launch fresh waves of missile strikes against Israeli, US targets Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army have announced the launch of fresh waves of massive missile strikes targeting strategic sites in the Israeli-occupied territories and US bases across the region.
  • US-allied fuel tanker attacked by Iran still burning in Strait of Hormuz: IRGC Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says a US-allied fuel tanker attacked in Iran's retaliatory drone strikes is still burning in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • IRGC Claims Strike on Netanyahu's Office Sputnik 02 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has claimed responsibility for a strike targeting the office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu earlier this morning.
  • Israeli PM's fate unclear after 'surprise' missile attack on his office: IRGC Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says the fate of Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu is uncertain following "purposeful and surprise" attacks on his office and the residence of the regime's air force commander.
  • Amid Iran's Leadership Wipeout, Ali Larijani Emerges As Key Player RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- As Iran reels from a US and Israeli bombing campaign that has killed several or perhaps even dozens of senior leaders, national security chief Ali Larijani has emerged as one of the key figures in what remains of the country's power structures.
  • Khamenei's Death A Major Blow But Collapse Of Iran's Clerical Establishment Not Guaranteed RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- The death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is a major blow to Iran's clerical rulers, but experts say there's no guarantee it will lead to the collapse of the Islamic republic.
  • Iran will hold no negotiations with US: Top security official Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) Ali Larijani says the Islamic Republic will hold no negotiations with the United States after Washington and Tel Aviv waged war against the country.
  • Attacks on hospitals, schools 'strike at life itself': Pezeshkian Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian condemned the US-Israeli strikes targeting the Gandhi Hospital in Tehran and an elementary school in the Hormozgan Province, stating that attacks on medical facilities strike "at life itself" and assaults on schools jeopardize "a nation's future."
  • Iran's attacks on US bases 'legitimate' response to source of aggression: Araghchi Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran is determined to defend its sovereignty and the security of its people, noting that its strikes on US military bases in the region "are a legitimate response" to the source of the ongoing US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran FM: Kuwait must answer what US aircraft were doing in its airspace if it downed them Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the country will submit an official note of protest to the Kuwaiti government following claims regarding the downing of three United States F-15 fighter jets.
  • US-Israeli attacks damage 5 hospitals, medical centers in Iran: MP Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- A member of the Iranian Parliament says five hospitals and medical centers have been damaged or destroyed during the US-Israeli terrorist attacks on the Islamic Republic.
  • Unlike US, Iran prepared for a long war: Security chief Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council (SNSC), Ali Larijani, says Iran did not initiate the current war, but it remains fully prepared to sustain a long-term conflict, unlike the United States.
  • Iran says has 'no choice' but to fight back, holds no enmity toward American people Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Iran's foreign ministry spokesman says confronting US-Israeli threats leaves the country no option but military retaliation, while stressing it has no issue with the American people.
  • Larijani Says 'Will Not Negotiate' With US As Trump Vows To Punish Iran With 'Full Force' RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- Powerful Iranian Supreme National Security Council chief Ali Larijani said Tehran "will not negotiate" with Washington, hours after US President Donald Trump vowed to continue to punish the "terrorists" with the "full force" of the American military.
  • Iran writes to UN, warns about dire consequences for perpetrators following Leader's martyrdom Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has formally addressed the United Nations following the martyrdom of Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, warning of "deep and far-reaching consequences" for those responsible.

  • Trump Says He Picked 3 Candidates to Lead Iran Sputnik 02 Mar 2026 -- Following the US attack, US President Donald Trump announced that he had three candidates in mind to lead Iran.
  • Iran posed no imminent threat to US: Pentagon tells Congress Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- As US-Israeli military aggression against Iran continues, Pentagon staff have acknowledged that there was no military threat from Tehran towards US bases and forces in West Asia prior to US aggression, contradicting claims by senior administration officials that Washington started the aggression to prevent Tehran from targeting American interests in the region.
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to Press US Dept. of State 02 Mar 2026
  • Democrats, Republicans divided over Trump's attack on Iran People's Daily 02 Mar 2026 -- Democratic and Republican lawmakers are deeply divided over U.S. President Donald Trump's attack on Iran, as Congress is expected to vote on war powers in the coming week.

  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Omani Foreign Minister Sayyid Badr bin Hamad bin Hamood Albusaidi PRC MofA 02 Mar 2026 -- China will take necessary measures to safeguard its own energy security, said Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning on Tuesday, in response to a reporter's question that some analysts and officials in Asia claimed that US actions in Venezuela and Iran could impact China's oil supply and weaken China's diplomatic partners.
  • Chinese FM Wang Yi holds phone talks with Iranian, French, Omani counterparts as US-Israel military operation enters 3rd day Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Monday held phone conversations separately with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi, French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot and Omani Foreign Minister Sayyid Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi as the military operation launched by the US and Israel on Iran entered its third day.
  • Chinese FM affirms support to Iran in safeguarding sovereignty, territorial integrity Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Monday said that China values the traditional friendship between China and Iran and supports Iran in safeguarding its sovereignty, security, territorial integrity and national dignity.
  • Chinese FM says spillover of war does not serve interests of Gulf countries Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Monday that the spillover of war does not serve the fundamental and long-term interests of the Gulf countries.
  • China firmly opposes, strongly condemns US-Israel attack that killed Iran's supreme leader: FM Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- In response to Japanese media inquiries regarding military strikes against Iran by the US and Israel that resulted in the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, Chinese FM spokesperson Mao Ning stated on Monday that the attack and killing of Iran's top leader constitute a serious violation of Iran's sovereignty and security, and a flagrant breach of the purposes and principles of the UN Charter and the basic norms of international relations. China firmly opposes and strongly condemns these acts. We urge the immediate cessation of military actions to prevent further escalation of tensions and to jointly safeguard peace and stability in the Middle East and the world at large, said Mao.
  • Chinese FM spokesperson on US military strikes against Iran to last 4 weeks: 'we urge all parties to cease military actions' Global Times 02 Mar 2026
  • China was not notified in advance regarding US military action, says FM on whether China had contact with US during or prior to US-Israeli strikes on Iran Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- China was not notified in advance regarding the US military action, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said, in response to a media inquiry about whether China had any contact with the US during or prior to the US and Israel's military strikes against Iran.
  • FM spokesperson vows enhanced measures to protect Chinese citizens in Iran as situation evolves Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- Since the tense security situation in Iran began, the Chinese Foreign Ministry and the Chinese Embassy and consulates in Iran have issued multiple reminders and carried out work in various forms, calling on and assisting Chinese citizens to evacuate as soon as possible, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at a press conference on Monday, in response to media inquiry over the measures that China would take in the next stage to protect Chinese citizens in Iran.
  • Chinese FM urges all parties to cease military actions, prevent further escalation of conflict in Middle East Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- The US and Israel carried out military strikes against Iran without authorization from the UN Security Council, which violates the international law. China is deeply concerned about the spillover of the conflict affecting neighboring countries.
  • Chinese FM refutes reports on Iran-China supersonic anti ship missile deal, dismisses claims as untrue and opposes malicious hype Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- When asked to confirm foreign media reports that Iran and China are close to reaching an agreement on the purchase of CM-302 supersonic anti ship missiles, which, if delivered, would be among the most advanced weapons China has provided to Iran in recent years, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at a press conference on Monday that the relevant reports are not true./li>
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi PRC MofA 02 Mar 2026 -- On March 2, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi upon request.
  • Major countries with military advantage must not arbitrarily attack other countries: Chinese FM Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Monday said that major countries must not use their military advantage to launch arbitrary attacks against other nations.
  • China condemns killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- China on Sunday strongly condemned the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, calling the move "a grave violation of Iran's sovereignty and security."

  • Statement of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization regarding the Situation around the Islamic Republic of Iran Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Ministry statement regarding recent developments concerning Iran Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Mar 2026
  • Moscow in Constant Contact With Leadership of Iran - Kremlin Sputnik 02 Mar 2026 -- Moscow is in constant contact with the leadership of Iran, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Monday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answers to questions from Izvestia regarding the situation with Russian citizens in Middle Eastern countries Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Mar 2026
  • Telephone conversation with King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain President of Russia 02 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain, which currently holds the presidency of the Gulf Cooperation Council.
  • Telephone conversation with Emir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani President of Russia 02 Mar 2026
  • Telephone conversation with UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan President of Russia 02 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the United Arab Emirates Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.

  • Iran crisis: Nuclear watchdog urges restraint amid ongoing strikes UN News 02 Mar 2026 -- As the violent escalation in the Middle East entered a third day on Monday sparked by the bombing of Iran by Israel and the United States, the head of the UN atomic energy agency urged a return to diplomacy, given the "increasing risk to nuclear safety" in the region.
    • Readout of the telephone conversation between the Secretary-General and His Highness Mr. Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, Amir of the State of Qatar UN Secretary-General 02 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR deeply concerned by Middle East escalation and impact on civilians UNHCR 02 Mar 2026 -- UNHCR is deeply concerned about the escalation of conflict in the Middle East and its impact on civilians and further displacement in the region.
  • IOM Chief Warns of Displacement Risk Amid Regional Escalation IOM 02 Mar 2026 -- The international community must act immediately to prevent further human suffering as tensions escalate in the Islamic Republic of Iran and across the Middle East region and beyond, where already fragile displacement situations risk deteriorating further, said Amy Pope, Director General of the International Organization for Migration (IOM).

  • UK Allowed US to Use Its Bases to Strike Missile Depots in Iran - Starmer Sputnik 02 Mar 2026 -- The United Kingdom has accepted a US request to use its military bases to strike missile depots in Iran, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced.
  • EU Statement On Iran Shows Its Diminished Role As Foreign Policy Player RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- The European Union's 27 member states issued a joint statement expressing "utmost concern" after the bloc's foreign ministers met online for nearly three hours to discuss the American-Israeli attack on Iran over the weekend and Tehran's subsequent military response on several countries in the region.
  • EU Statement on Agenda item 1 at Extraordinary Board of Governors - International Atomic Energy Agency European External Action Service (EEAS) 02 Mar 2026
  • B. Braže at EU Foreign Affairs Council meeting: Iran has repeatedly violated its international commitments and for years has acted as a malign and destabilising force in the region and globally, including by supporting Russia's aggression against Ukraine Latvia MFA 02 Mar 2026
  • Spanish MFA summons Iranian Ambassador to Spain Spain MFA 02 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, European Union and Cooperation reports that the Iranian Ambassador to Spain was summoned today to convey Spain's strongest protest against the attacks launched against European territory.
  • Prime Minister's Oral Statement on Iran: 2 March 2026 Gov.UK 02 Mar 2026
  • Phone conversation between the Foreign Ministers of Armenia and Iran Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 02 Mar 2026
  • Statement by Minister Anand on hostilities in the Middle East Canada 02 Mar 2026
  • Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union on developments in the Middle East European External Action Service (EEAS) 02 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in telephone conversations with his UAE, Omani and Iraqi counterparts: we are acting to ensure the safety of our citizens Estonia MFA 02 Mar 2026 -- Today, 2 March, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna held telephone conversations with the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the Foreign Minister of Oman, Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi, and the Foreign Minister of Iraq, Fuad Hussein, to discuss developments in the Middle East, the safety of Estonian citizens in the region and possibilities for their return home.
  • Statements by the Government Spokesman of the Republic of Cyprus after the extraordinary meeting of the National Council, 2 March 2026 Republic of Cyprus 02 Mar 2026 -- Our absolute and overriding priority is to ensure the security of our country and our citizens, Government Spokesman, Mr Konstantinos Letymbiotis, said today, 2 March 2026.
  • Message by the President of the Republic of Cyprus, Mr Nikos Christodoulides Republic of Cyprus 02 Mar 2026
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide on the war in the Middle East Government of Norway 02 Mar 2026
  • MOFA Spokesperson's Statement on Situation in Middle East ROK MFA 02 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister MOTEGI's Meetings with Ambassadors of Middle Eastern Countries to Japan Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 02 Mar 2026
  • League's Secretary-General Aboul Gheit condemns Iranian attacks on Oman's Port of Duqm and oil tanker off its coast League of Arab States 02 Mar 2026
  • President Marcos continues to monitor Middle East tension - Palace Philippine News Agency 02 Mar 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. is closely monitoring developments in the Middle East amid the ongoing conflict, a Malacañang official said Monday.
  • "Our priority is to establish ceasefire and clear the path for dialogue" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 02 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at an iftar dinner with AK Party's CDAC members, President Erdoğan said: "Our priority is to establish ceasefire and clear the path for dialogue. I am speaking frankly; this conflict-ridden process will have severe implications for regional and global security if the necessary intervention is not undertaken. No one could bear the economic and geopolitical uncertainties that would result from such a process. The fire, therefore, must be extinguished before it spreads even further."
  • Andrii Sybiha held phone talks with Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the UAE H.H. Sheikh Abdullah Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 02 Mar 2026
  • Prime Minister speaks with the King of Jordan India PIB 02 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with His Majesty King Abdullah II, the King of Jordan
  • Prime Minister holds discussions with the Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia India PIB 02 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held discussions with His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia
  • Prime Minister holds a telephone conversation with The King of Bahrain India PIB 02 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a telephone conversation today with His Majesty King Hamad Bin Isa Al Khalifa, the King of Bahrain.
  • The Prime Minister speaks with the Prime Minister of Israel India PIB 02 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a telephone conversation with the Prime Minister of Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu, to discuss the current regional situation

  • Telephone conversation with Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud President of Russia 02 Mar 2026 -- During Vladimir Putin's telephone conversation with Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud, the leaders had a thorough discussion of the escalation in the Middle East resulting from the United States and Israel's armed aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Saudi Arabian Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Mar 2026

  • Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, UAE, US Yesterday Issue Statement on Iran's Missile and Drone Attacks in the Region SPA 02 Mar 2026 -- A joint statement was issued yesterday on Iran's missile and drone attacks in the region, the text of which is as follows:
  • Ministry of Energy Official Source: A Limited Fire at the Ras Tanura Refinery Brought Under Control Following Debris from Intercepted Drones, With No Injuries or Impact on Supplies SPA 02 Mar 2026 -- An official source at the Ministry of Energy stated that at 7:04 a.m. on Monday, the Ras Tanura oil refinery sustained limited damage as a result of debris from the interception of two drones in its vicinity. The incident resulted in a limited fire, which was immediately contained by emergency response teams. No injuries or fatalities were reported

  • Khamenei's Removal Could Reshape Iran: General Petraeus Explains The Risks RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- Retired four-star General David Petraeus, a veteran of nearly four decades in the US Army and a former director of the CIA, endorses the Trump administration's frank account of operations.
  • US Strategy In Iran: Create An Opening, Let Iranians Decide, Analysts Say RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- As the war with Iran enters a volatile new phase, analysts in Washington say the Islamic Republic is confronting a convergence of crises unseen since its founding in 1979: the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei amid a sustained US-Israeli air campaign, and the possibility of internal unrest that could reshape the political order.
  • Analyst: US plans weeks-long assault on Iran, but can its ammunition stockpile sustain it? Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- As Washington signaled that its conflict with Iran could last up to four weeks on Sunday local time, media scrutiny over US ammunition stockpiles has risen. A Chinese military affairs expert told the Global Times that although the US made relatively large-scale preparations for its strikes on Iran, limited ammunition reserves and declining defense industrial capacity may constrain its ability to sustain a prolonged high-intensity war.
  • Timeline: U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran trigger escalation, Khamenei killed People's Daily 02 Mar 2026 -- A joint military strike by the United States and Israel on Iran, which started on Saturday morning, triggered a sharp escalation across the Middle East, setting off waves of missile exchanges, mounting casualties, and far-reaching political and security repercussions.
  • Khamenei's killing sparks concerns over prolonged regional chaos People's Daily 02 Mar 2026 -- Iran confirmed on Sunday that its Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli airstrikes the previous day.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Army Strategic Capital Initiatives - Seeking private sector partners to accelerate enterprise-wide modernization US Army 02 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Army has announced a major initiative to partner with the private sector, seeking to harness the speed and ingenuity of the commercial market to accelerate a historic, enterprise-wide modernization effort. Through a formal request for information issued today, the Army is inviting industry leaders, investors, and innovators to help build the future Army by co-investing in its installations, technology, energy infrastructure, and industrial base.
  • Cobra Gold 2026 Strengthens Crisis Response Through Joint Multinational Noncombatant Evacuation Operation Training US Navy 02 Mar 2026 -- U.S. joint services and multinational partners conducted a simulated noncombatant evacuation operation (NEO) during Exercise Cobra Gold 2026 at U-Tapao Royal Thai Navy Airport, Thailand, Feb. 26, 2026.

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on February 23 - March 2, 2026 Lithuania MND 02 Mar 2026

  • Supply, loss audit, and scaling unmanned ground robotic systems: Ministry of Defence advances solutions for the front line Ukraine MOD 02 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defence streamlines weapons codification and accelerates the supply of new developments to the military Ukraine MOD 02 Mar 2026

  • Product of Concern Summit for Collins class sustainment Australia DOD 02 Mar 2026 -- The Albanese Government held its fourth Product of Concern Summit in Canberra today to support the sustainment of Australia's Collins class submarines.

  • Charges laid in relation to the loss of HMNZS Manawanui New Zealand Defence Force 02 Mar 2026 -- Charges have been laid in the Court Martial of New Zealand in relation to the loss of Royal New Zealand Navy vessel HMNZS Manawanui, off the coast of Samoa in October 2024.
  • Royal New Zealand Navy strengthens bonds with Singaporean counterparts New Zealand Defence Force 02 Mar 2026 -- The strong partnership between New Zealand and Singapore's maritime forces has been reinforced, through a week-long visit by 21 Republic of Singapore Navy (RSN) personnel.
  • C-130J Simulator nears next development milestone New Zealand Defence Force 02 Mar 2026 -- Systems power up, visuals are in place and the engine roars in the new C-130J Hercules simulator, installed at Royal New Zealand Air Force (RNZAF) Base Auckland.

  • Speech by the President of the Republic on France's nuclear deterrence. Élysée - President of France 02 Mar 2026
  • Joint declaration of President Macron and Chancellor Merz. Élysée - President of France 02 Mar 2026 -- In the spirit of their close partnership as set out in Article 4 of the Treaty of Aachen, France and Germany have decided to enter into closer cooperation in the field of deterrence in response to the evolving threat landscape. France and Germany have established a high-ranking nuclear steering group that will act as a bilateral framework for doctrinal dialogue and the coordination of strategic cooperation, including consultations regarding the appropriate mix of conventional, missile defence and French nuclear capabilities.

  • Billion-pound helicopter deal secures 3,300 British jobs, boosts battlefield kit and unlocks up to £15 billion in UK exports UK MOD 02 Mar 2026 -- Leonardo UK selected for £1 billion contract for new medium helicopters (NMH) - which could work alongside uncrewed aircraft - to support defence operations around the globe.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the Ambassador of the United Kingdom of Great Britain to Bucharest Romania - Ministry of National Defence 02 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, March 2, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Ambassador of the United Kingdom of Great Britain to Romania, H.E. Giles Portman, at the MoND headquarters.

  • Defense official: EDCA sites under PH control, not American bases Philippine News Agency 02 Mar 2026 -- The Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA) sites in the Philippines pose no threat to any country, the spokesperson of the Department of National Defense (DND) said on Monday.

    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 2 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 02 Mar 2026
  • With 473 Million Children Living in or Fleeing Conflict Zones, Speakers in Security Council Advocate Digital Education to Ensure Learning UN Security Council 02 Mar 2026 -- Digital education, when designed with robust safeguards, can ensure children can access learning even when conflict shuts down schools, the Security Council heard today at its first March meeting under the United States presidency of that body.

  • Israeli Army Announces Beginning of Strikes Against Hezbollah Targets in Lebanon Sputnik 02 Mar 2026 -- The Israeli army has announced the beginning of strikes against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon.
  • At least 31 killed in Israeli aggression on southern Lebanon after Hezbollah strikes Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- At least 31 people, most of them women and children, were killed in attacks by Israel across southern Lebanon following Hezbollah's entry into the front line in defense of Iran.
  • Hezbollah strikes occupied Haifa in retaliation for Leader's assassination Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah has struck a military target in the city of Haifa in the occupied territories with a rocket barrage and a drone swarm in retaliation for the martyrdom of Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei by the United States and the Israeli regime.

  • UN experts condemn 'Board of Peace', call for a reparative, rights-based approach to reconstruction in Gaza OHCHR 02 Mar 2026 -- The international community must ensure that any efforts to reconstruct Gaza are guided by international law and human rights norms and standards, rather than by neo-colonial interests that rest on impunity and avarice, UN experts* said today.

  • Iran slams Israeli attacks on Lebanon, warn UNSC's inaction to embolden occupying regime Press TV 02 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei has denounced Israel's latest attacks on Lebanon, saying the United Nations Security Council's inaction on the occupying regime's crimes will discredit the world body.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Middle East, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan UNOCHA 02 Mar 2026

  • PH to continue using transparency policy vs. China's gray-zone tactics Philippine News Agency 02 Mar 2026 -- National Security Adviser (NSA) Eduardo Año said the Philippines will continue using its transparency policy to counter China's gray-zone activities in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).
  • Philippine vessel's risky maneuver near Huangyan Dao 'highlights rising provocations' Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- In the blue waters of the South China Sea, a China Coast Guard (CCG) vessel was seen cruising at high speed when, from its port bow, a Philippine ship altered course to the right, cutting across the front of the Chinese vessel at close range, disregarding the risk of a potentially fatal collision. The scene comes from exclusive footage obtained by the Global Times on Sunday of the incident that occurred on Friday.

  • Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for South Asia, Sanjay Wijesekera, on the impact of renewed cross-border hostilities on children in Afghanistan and Pakistan UNICEF 02 Mar 2026

  • The Kingdom of Belgium reaffirmed on Monday its clear and consistent support for the Autonomy Initiative under Moroccan sovereignty, considering it "the most adequate, serious, credible, and realistic basis" for achieving a fair, lasting, and mutually acceptable political solution to the regional dispute over the Sahara. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Mar 2026

  • Gov't, MILF hold 'amnesty intake activity' in Mamasapano Philippine News Agency 02 Mar 2026 -- The Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity (OPAPRU) on Monday said members of the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) took part in an "on-site amnesty intake activity" under the Normalization Track of the Comprehensive Agreement on the Bangsamoro (CAB) last week.

  • Sanctioning Rwandan Violators of the Washington Accords for Peace and Prosperity US Dept. of State 02 Mar 2026 -- Today, the United States is sanctioning the Rwandan Defense Force (RDF) and four senior RDF officers for their direct operational support to the March 23 Movement (M23) and its affiliates in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).
  • Treasury Sanctions Rwanda Officials, Condemns Blatant Violations of Washington Peace Accords US Treasury 02 Mar 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on the Rwanda Defence Force (RDF)—the military of Rwanda—and four of its senior officials. The RDF is actively supporting, training, and fighting alongside the March 23 Movement (M23), a U.S.- and United Nations (UN)-sanctioned armed group responsible for human rights abuses and a mass displacement crisis in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).
  • Risch Statement on Rwanda Sanctions US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 02 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Senator Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, today released the statement below regarding newly announced U.S. sanctions on Rwanda.

  • UN Human Rights Council 61: Core Group Statement on South Sudan UK FCDO 02 Mar 2026 -- Core Group Statement on South Sudan. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on South Sudan UK FCDO 02 Mar 2026 -- Item 2: Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on the report of the Commission of Human Rights in South Sudan. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

News Reports

  • IAEA Director General's Introductory Statement to the Board of Governors IAEA 02 Mar 2026

  • Meeting with Amur Region Governor Vasily Orlov President of Russia 02 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Governor of the Amur Region Vasily Orlov. The discussion focused on the region's socioeconomic development, including the situation in agriculture, road and housing construction, as well as energy, transport, and infrastructure projects. Special attention was given to expanding cross-border cooperation.

  • China's top political advisory body concludes standing committee session PRC MND 02 Mar 2026 -- The 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), China's top political advisory body, closed the 15th session of its standing committee on Monday in Beijing.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on March 2, 2026 PRC MofA 02 Mar 2026
  • Chinese FM responds to US reported plans for AI-powered cyber reconnaissance against China Global Times 02 Mar 2026
  • China Weighs Its Options As Iran Strikes Upend The Middle East RFE/RL 02 Mar 2026 -- China has escalated its diplomatic rhetoric following US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei while moving cautiously to navigate disruption to global oil markets and the threat of broader instability in the Middle East.
  • Chinese FM holds phone talks with Russian counterpart over U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran People's Daily 02 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Sunday held a phone conversation with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov at the latter's request.
  • China urges UK to immediately correct wrongdoings after London imposes sanctions on Chinese companies over Russia Global Times 02 Mar 2026 -- China strongly deplores and firmly rejects the UK's unilateral sanctions that lack any basis in international law and are not authorized by the UN Security Council, a spokesperson from China's Ministry of Commerce said on Monday in response to the UK Foreign Office's February 24 announcement of a new round of Russia-related sanctions, involving multiple Chinese companies.

  • Iran could cloud Trump-Xi talks but unlikely to spark Taiwan war: Scholars CNA 02 Mar 2026 -- Escalating conflict in the Middle East is unlikely to trigger an immediate cross-strait crisis or directly imperil Taiwan in U.S.-China dealings, but it could pose growing risks by straining U.S. resources and complicating the upcoming Trump-Xi meeting, scholars said.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 02 Mar 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Pays Congratulatory Visit to Sangwon Cement Complex KCNA 02 Mar 2026 -- The historic Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea unfolded a grand plan to further expand the construction work for the comprehensive development of the state and the promotion of the welfare of the people throughout the country during the new long-term plan and ardently appealed to all the relevant units, including building-materials production bases, to bring about a surge in production, surpassing the one of the past five years.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Sangwon Cement Complex KCNA 02 Mar 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the Sangwon Cement Complex.

  • List of Outcomes: Visit of Prime Minister of Canada to India India PIB 02 Mar 2026
  • Minister Anand met with India's External Affairs Minister Canada 02 Mar 2026 -- Yesterday, the Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, met with Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, India's Minister of External Affairs, during her visit to India with Prime Minister Mark Carney.
  • Joint statement by Prime Minister Carney and Prime Minister Modi Prime Minister of Canada 02 Mar 2026
